meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve stuck with basic apps
content.seo_description: Learn how to optimize SaaS pricing and overcome being stuck with basic apps. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid development.
content.answer_block: Stop building static CRUD apps and start engineering profitable SaaS ecosystems. By integrating dynamic pricing models directly into your codebase using Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform from a task-based freelancer into a high-value architect. This approach automates revenue logic, ensuring your clients see immediate ROI from your builds.
content.intro:
The cold reality of freelancing is that basic CRUD apps are a commodity. You are likely stuck in a cycle of low-margin builds, competing on price rather than value. When you stop viewing yourself as a coder and start viewing yourself as a SaaS architect, you break the cycle of stagnation.

True velocity comes from automating the complex business logic that clients fear. By leveraging advanced tooling like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can inject sophisticated SaaS pricing strategies into any project in minutes. This isn't just about writing code; it's about building a scalable engine that justifies premium rates and keeps your pipeline full.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Pricing Logic
steps.description: Establish the business rules for your SaaS tiers. We need to define feature gates and usage limits before writing any code to ensure the architecture is sound.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust SaaS pricing schema in TypeScript that handles three tiers: Basic, Pro, and Enterprise, including feature flagging support and Stripe integration hooks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file pricing_schema.ts --context "Use the generated schema to scaffold the pricing model structure."

steps.title: Implement Usage-Based Metering
steps.description: Modern SaaS requires metering. We will hook into your database to track consumption, which directly influences your pricing logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware function that checks current user usage against their plan limits and throws a custom 'UpgradeRequired' exception if limits are exceeded.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file middleware/pricing_guard.ts --apply "Implement the usage check logic."

steps.title: Automate Stripe Checkout Integration
steps.description: Bridge the gap between your app and the payment gateway. We automate the checkout session creation to reduce manual overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a backend controller that initializes a Stripe Checkout session based on the user's selected SaaS tier, including success and cancel URL handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file controllers/payment.controller.ts --prompt "Integrate Stripe API for tiered subscription checkout."

steps.title: Build the Dynamic Pricing Dashboard
steps.description: Give the client a view of their subscription status. This adds immense value and professionalism to your final deliverable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component that displays the current subscription tier and a 'Upgrade' call-to-action button, fetching data from the API built in Step 3.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file components/PricingStatus.tsx --context "Build a clean, responsive UI for subscription management."

steps.title: Verify and Deploy
steps.description: Run a final audit on your pricing logic to ensure no edge cases exist where a user gains premium features for free.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a security audit on the pricing middleware, specifically checking for race conditions or bypass vulnerabilities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test --run --all --context "Verify all pricing guardrails and checkout flows."

pro_tips: Always wrap your pricing logic in a dedicated service layer so you can swap Stripe for Paddle or LemonSqueezy without rewriting the frontend.
Use 'Feature Flagging' as a service: tie your UI components to the pricing tier constant rather than individual feature checks.
Implement 'Grace Periods' in your database schema to handle failed payments without instantly locking out client users.
Inject a 'Referral Code' discount field into your checkout session early; clients love this feature and it costs you 5 minutes to add.
Never hardcode prices. Always fetch the current pricing configuration from a secure environment variable or a remote config service like LaunchDarkly.
content.next_steps: Now that your pricing architecture is live, focus on building a 'Usage Analytics' dashboard to show your clients exactly how much value their users are deriving from your app. This justifies your recurring maintenance fees.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for basic apps and start architecting the future of SaaS. Our community of elite builders is waiting to help you scale your freelance business to the next level. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
